TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) Congestion Control is a mechanism used to manage the amount of data that can be sent over a network in real-time. It's designed to prevent the network from becoming overwhelmed with too much traffic and ensure that all packets arrive at their destination without being lost or delayed.

The basic idea behind TCP Congestion Control is to maintain a buffer on the server side for incoming connections. When a connection is established, the server sends a header containing information about the size of the packet it will send next.
